title: Sankatmochan Hanuman Ashtak
intro: Read Sankatmochan Hanuman Ashtak with Hindi meaning. Chant to seek the divine blessings of Lord Hanuman.
description:
Baal samay ravi bhaksh liyo tab, teenhu lok bhayo andhiyaro |
Taahi so traas bhayo jag ko, yeh sankat kaahu so jaat na taaro ||
Devan aani kari binti tab, chhaadi diyo ravi kasht nivaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Baali ki traas kapees basein giri, jaat mahaprabhu panth nihaaro |
Chaunki mahamuni saap diyo tab, chahiye kaun bichaar bichaaro ||
Kaidwij roop livaay mahaprabhu, so tum daas ke shok nivaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Angad ke sang len gaye siy, khoj kapees yeh bain uchaaro |
Jeevat na bachihau hum so ju, bina sudhi laaye ihaapagu dhaaro ||
Heri thake tat sindhu sabe tab, laaye siya-sudhi praan ubaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Raavan traas daai siy ko sab, raakshasi so kahi shok nivaaro |
Taahi samay hanumaan mahaprabhu, jaaye maha rajneechar maaro ||
Chaahat siy ashok so aagi su, dai prabhu mudrika shok nivaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Baan laagyo ur lachhman ke tab, praan taje soot raavan maaro |
Lai grih baidy sushen samet, tabai giri dron su beer upaaro ||
Aani sajeevan haath diye tab, lachhman ke tum praan ubaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Raavan judh ajaan kiyo tab, naag ki phaans sabai sir daaro |
Shreeraghunaath samet sabai dal, moh bhayo yeh sankat bhaaro ||
Aani khages tabai hanumaan ju, bandhan kaati sutraas nivaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Bandhu samet jabai ahiraavan, lai ragunaath paataal sidhaaro |
Debinhin pooji bhali vidhi so bali, deu sabai mili mantra bichaaro ||
Jaaye sahaa-e bhayo tab hi, ahiraavan sainya samet sanhaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Kaaj kiye bad devan ke tum, beer mahaprabhu dekhi bichaaro |
Kaun so sankat mor gareeb ko, jo tumse nahin jaat hai taaro ||
Begi haro hanumaan mahaprabhu, jo kachhu sankat hoye hamaaro |
Ko nahi jaanat hai jag mein kapi, sankat mochan naam tihaaro ||

Doha
Laal deh laali lase, aru dhari laal langoor |
Vajra deh daanav dalan, jay jay jay kapi soor ||
Jay ShriRam, Jay Hanuman, Jay Hanuman.

faq.q1: What is the Sankat Mochan Hanuman Ashtak?
faq.a1: The Sankat Mochan Hanuman Ashtak is a devotional hymn dedicated to Lord Hanuman. It was composed by Tulsidas, the famous saint and author of the Ramcharitmanas. The word "Sankat Mochan" means "Reliever of Troubles."
faq.q2: When should one recite the Hanuman Ashtak?
faq.a2: It is primarily recited to overcome major obstacles, fears, and crises in life. Many devotees recite it on Tuesdays and Saturdays, or during times of distress when they need divine strength and intervention.
faq.q3: What are the benefits of reciting this hymn?
faq.a3:
The Ashtak is believed to provide immense mental and spiritual relief. Reciting it is said to:
Help remove obstacles and negative energies.
Protect the devotee from fear and anxiety.
Grant mental strength and courage to face difficult life situations.
Fulfill desires and help achieve success in arduous tasks.
faq.q4: Are there any specific rules for recitation?
faq.a4: There are no rigid rules. However, it is recommended to maintain cleanliness, sit in a peaceful place, and recite with sincere devotion and focus. Maintaining a Sattvic (pure) lifestyle during the period of recitation is often encouraged by devotees.
faq.a5: "Sankat" refers to problems or crises, and "Mochan" refers to the one who releases or solves them. Since Lord Hanuman is famously known for helping Lord Rama overcome immense challenges (like finding Sita, saving Lakshmana), he is addressed as the "Sankat Mochan"—the one who liberates his devotees from their troubles.
